title: Mathematical Literacy
description: Mathematical Literacy in Grade 12, according to the CAPS document, equips learners with essential skills to engage confidently with real-life situations involving numerical and spatial concepts. The curriculum focuses on applying mathematical techniques and non-mathematical considerations to understand and solve problems in diverse authentic contexts. Learners develop critical thinking, problem-solving, and communication abilities crucial for everyday living and informed decision-making.

topics.title: Working with Two or More Relationships
topics.description: Learners analyze and represent scenarios involving multiple related variables, often using graphs and equations to compare different systems like electricity costs.

topics.title: Financial Documents
topics.description: Learners interpret and apply information from various financial documents, including guides related to taxation, to understand personal and business finance.

topics.title: Banking, Loans and Investments
topics.description: This topic focuses on understanding different banking services, modeling loan scenarios, and evaluating various investment opportunities.

topics.title: Taxation
topics.description: Learners acquire skills in calculating personal income tax, understanding tax brackets, and analyzing the impact of taxation on individuals' financial situations.

topics.title: Exchange Rates
topics.description: Learners utilize exchange rates to convert currencies, plan for international transactions, and assess the financial implications of global travel.

topics.title: Measuring Mass (Weight)
topics.description: This topic involves investigating and interpreting mass measurements, often in health-related contexts such as assessing the weight status of children.

topics.title: Perimeter, Area and Volume
topics.description: Learners apply calculations of perimeter, area, and volume to practical problems such as tiling projects and determining housing density.

topics.title: Planning a Trip (Time, Maps, Finance)
topics.description: Learners integrate understanding of time management, map interpretation, and financial calculations to plan and budget for travel.

topics.title: Plans, Models, Perimeter, Area, Volume, Finance
topics.description: Learners apply concepts from plans, models, and measurements of perimeter, area, and volume, integrated with financial considerations, to projects like building a house.

topics.title: Models
topics.description: This topic focuses on constructing and interpreting models of structures, applying scale and spatial reasoning to understand their real-world counterparts.

topics.title: Data Handling
topics.description: Learners develop comprehensive skills in all aspects of data handling, including collecting, organizing, representing, and analyzing data to draw conclusions and inform decisions, often in contexts such as risky behaviour.

topics.title: Probability
topics.description: This topic enables learners to understand and apply the principles of probability to real-world situations, assessing likelihoods and making informed judgments based on chance.
